etymology.description: 往(往) (wǎng, "past; toward") refers to the direction of going away, or the time that has passed, emphasizing what has been and is no more. 事(事) (shì, "affair; event; matter") denotes occurrences or activities. Thus, 往事(往事) (wǎngshì, "bygones; past events") combines these elements to refer to events or matters of the past.
etymology.interpretation: The word 往事(往事) (wǎngshì, "bygones; past events") captures the sense of looking back at the 'affairs or events' (事) that belong to the 'past' (往). Anything that once occurred is considered a 'past event,' emphasizing the separation between then and now.

examples.chinese: 他常常回忆往事(他常常回憶往事)。
examples.english: He often reminisces about past events.

examples.chinese: 记住往事(記住往事)，才能更好地走向未来(才能更好地走向未來)。
examples.pinyin: Jìzhu wǎngshì, cáinéng gèng hǎo de zǒuxiàng wèilái.
examples.english: Remembering the past helps to better move towards the future.

examples.chinese: 往事已成过眼云烟(往事已成過眼雲煙)。
examples.english: Past events have already become fleeting clouds.
